usage_notes: Universal moisturizer. Safe for all skin types and ages.
Use on damp skin to avoid drawing moisture from dermis. Multiple MW versions beneficial.
Smaller MW than HA; penetrates deeper into skin layers.
Can irritate sensitive skin at higher concentrations. Patch test recommended.
Gentler than propylene glycol. Good for sensitive skin.
Can be mildly comedogenic at high concentrations in occlusive formulas.
Pro-Vitamin B5. Excellent wound healing and barrier support.
Low % (2-10%) moisturizing; high % (20-40%) keratolytic. Avoid broken skin.
Naturally found in skin. Excellent NMF component.
Derived from sugar beet. Excellent for sensitive/rosacea skin.
Protects skin proteins during environmental stress.
Non-comedogenic. Excellent for all skin types including acne-prone.
Technically a wax ester. Mimics sebum. Good for oily/acne skin.
High in linoleic acid. May not suit acne-prone skin. Check individual tolerance.
Rich in Vitamin E and fatty acids. Moderate comedogenicity.
Avoid with nut allergies. Comedogenicity rating 2.
Highly comedogenic (4). Best used on body, not face for acne-prone.
Comedogenicity 0. High linoleic acid. Excellent for acne and sensitive skin.
Unrefined may cause breakouts. Refined shea better for acne-prone.
Non-comedogenic silicone. Great primer/base ingredient. Safe for sensitive skin.
Fatty alcohol, not drying alcohol. Non-irritating.
Comedogenicity 3. Avoid in acne-prone skin. Penetration enhancer.
Well-refined mineral oil safe; heavy application may increase acne risk.
Best occlusives known. Not comedogenic per research but may trap sebum.
Low pH required (2.5-3.5). Unstable; use fresh. Avoid mixing with niacinamide simultaneously.
Stable form of Vitamin C. Works at higher pH. Good for sensitive skin.
Converts to ascorbic acid in skin. Stable and gentle.
Very stable. Oil and water compatible. Effective brightener.

avoid_combining_with: AHAs/BHAs (avoid same time)|benzoyl peroxide|vitamin C (avoid same time)
usage_notes: START low and slow. Use PM only. Always SPF. Not for pregnancy. Purging possible. Avoid with other actives until skin adapted.
Gentler retinoid. Less effective than retinol but more tolerable.
11x more potent than retinol. Gentler than tretinoin. PM only. Start slow.
Next-gen retinoid. Gentler than retinol with comparable efficacy. Better for sensitive.
Safe retinol alternative. Can use AM/PM. Pregnancy safe. Can combine with retinoids.
Multitasking ingredient. 5-10% optimal. May cause flushing at very high %%.
Effective brightener. More stable than beta arbutin. Pregnancy caution due to hydroquinone parent.
Can be irritating. Use with caution on sensitive skin. Limit to 2%.
Very well tolerated. Excellent for PIH and melasma.
Safe in pregnancy at OTC %. Excellent for rosacea and PIH.

avoid_combining_with: retinol (same time)|vitamin C (same time)|BHA (same time at high %)
usage_notes: Smallest AHA molecule, deepest penetration. Start 5-10%. Always SPF. Not in pregnancy.
Gentler than glycolic. Moisturizing properties. Better for dry/sensitive. Some prefer in pregnancy.
Largest AHA molecule, gentlest. Good for acne-prone and sensitive skin.

avoid_combining_with: retinol (same time at high %)|vitamin C (low pH)
usage_notes: Oil-soluble, penetrates pores. Best for acne/oily skin. Avoid in pregnancy. Can be drying.
Gentlest exfoliant. Antioxidant. Excellent for sensitive skin. Safe in pregnancy.
Dual exfoliant and antimicrobial. Safe in pregnancy. Excellent for sensitive skin.
Best for sensitive skin. Anti-inflammatory. Non-comedogenic. Safe in pregnancy.
Physical UV filter. Very safe. May leave white cast. Not irritating.
Unstable alone; needs photostabilizer. Avoid in pregnancy.
Photostabilizes avobenzone. Can cause contact dermatitis in some.
Photostable. Excellent broad-spectrum. Not approved in US.
Comedogenicity 2. Pure vitamin E. Synergistic with Vitamin C.
Esterified form. More stable but must convert in skin. Less potent than pure tocopherol.
Pairs well with Vitamin C. Photounstable; use PM.
Decreases with age. Good for energy-depleted mature skin.
Dramatically stabilizes Vitamins C and E. Best in combination.
Matrixyl. Effective at very low concentrations. Very well tolerated.
Argireline. Topical muscle relaxer. Best at expression lines.
GHK-Cu. Do not use same session as pure vitamin C or retinoids.
Essential for skin barrier. Works best with cholesterol and fatty acids.
Works synergistically with other ceramides.
Excellent for acne-prone and compromised barrier skin.
Critical skin barrier lipid. Forms ideal ratio with ceramides and fatty acids.
Excellent for sensitive and compromised skin. Wound healing.
Derived from chamomile. Excellent for inflamed/sensitive skin.
Cica. Excellent for compromised barrier, eczema, rosacea, post-procedure.
Base of many soothing products. Excellent first-aid ingredient.
Colloidal oat is FDA approved for eczema. Excellent for sensitive skin.
Glabridin is key brightening component. Good for melasma.
EGCG is primary active. Good for acne and anti-aging.
Very effective for P. acnes. Oxidizing agent. Bleaches fabric. Not for sensitive/dry.
Always dilute. Can cause contact dermatitis. Effective against P. acnes.
3rd gen retinoid. OTC 0.1%. Gentler than tretinoin. Not for pregnancy.
Gentle antimicrobial. Good for mild-moderate acne. Some odor.
Use alcohol-free version for sensitive skin. Can be drying.
Controls sebum production. Good for oily/acne-prone.
FDA approved for anti-wrinkle claims. Very well tolerated.
Good for eye area puffiness and dark circles. Antioxidant.
Powerful antioxidant. Good in PM routine.
Holds 5000x its weight in water. More effective than HA.
Film-forming surface moisture. Complements low MW HA.
Water-soluble UVB filter. Works well in lightweight formulas.
DHHB. Photostable UVA filter. Not yet approved in US.
Generally safe. Some reports of sensitivity in babies. EU limit 1%.
Multi-functional. Gentle. Supports phenoxyethanol.
Papaya enzyme. Gentle exfoliation. Heat/acid sensitive.
Pineapple enzyme. Anti-inflammatory properties.
Bacillus-derived enzyme. Very gentle. Good for sensitive skin exfoliation.
